content: Vaccine effectiveness assessment: 1) Clinical efficacy from randomized trials; 2) Real-world effectiveness from observational studies; 3) Immunogenicity correlates (antibody titers, cellular immunity). Serologic testing for immunity: indicated for select situations (HBsAb post-vaccination in healthcare workers, varicella IgG in uncertain history, measles/rubella titers in pregnancy screening). Not routinely recommended for most vaccines due to good correlation between documented vaccination and protection. Vaccine failure: primary (no seroconversion) vs secondary (waning immunity). Outbreak settings may warrant revaccination regardless of prior status (e.g., mumps third dose). Adverse event monitoring: VAERS (passive reporting), Vaccine Safety Datalink (active surveillance); serious adverse events extremely rare compared to disease risks.
